title: SAP Senior Supply Chain konsult
description:
Tillverkande företag tappar pengar varje dag på ineffektiva supply chain-flöden i form av tex för långa ledtider, dålig lagerstyrning eller bristande synlighet mellan inköp, lager och försäljning. Du vet hur man löser det både i processen och i systemet.


Det är den erfarenheten vi letar efter.

**Om rollen**


Som konsult hos oss jobbar du nära kunden med att få deras flöden att fungera i praktiken, inte bara på en PowerPoint.


Du förstår hur kundorder, inköp, lager och leverans hänger ihop, och hur det ska sättas upp i SAP S/4HANA för att ge effekt. Ibland handlar det om att designa rätt lösning från början, ibland om att rätta till sådant som inte riktigt fungerar.


Du växlar mellan att diskutera lösningar med verksamheten och att själv vara inne i detaljerna –konfigurera, testa och säkerställa att det faktiskt fungerar i vardagen.

**Exempel på uppdrag du kan komma att jobba med**

* Driva S/4HANA-konverteringar hos tillverkande kunder, ansvara för lösningsdesign och konfiguration inom försäljning och logistik
* Genomföra greenfield-implementationer av SAP S/4HANA med fullt ansvar för inköps- och säljflöden, datamigrering och go-live
* Implementera SAP Cloud ERP för kunder med komplexa organisationsstrukturer, exempelvis uppbyggnad av Shared Service Center med inköp och försäljning över många bolag
* Leda globala SAP-utrullningar med stöd till lokala verksamheter inom krav, test och go-live inom tillverknings- och fordonsindustrin
* Designa och förbättra inköpsflöden för kunder med höga krav på compliance, godkännandeflöden och säkerhet
* Arbeta nära kundens verksamhet som business analyst – förstå deras utmaningar och omsätta dem i SAP-lösningar som faktiskt används
* Sätta standarden för kundens SAP-processer och dokumentera dem i verktyg som tex Signavio

**Vi tror att du**

* Har gedigen erfarenhet av supply chain –försäljning, inköp, lager och logistik
* Är van att arbeta i SAP S/4HANA och förstår hur lösningar sätts upp i systemet
* Har ett starkt systemintresse och gillar att få detaljerna att fungera i praktiken
* Förstår hur processer hänger ihop tvärs modulerna SD, MM/IM, EWM/WM
* Har varit med i implementationer och vet vad som brukar strula (och hur man löser det)
* Trivs i dialog med kund och kan förklara komplexa saker på ett enkelt sätt
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska
* Är modig, driven och prestigelös

**Varför Stretch Evolve?**


Hos oss försvinner du inte i mängden. Vi är ett partnerägt bolag med en platt organisation, din röst hörs och du kan påverka på riktigt: i kundprojekt, affärsutveckling och vart vi är på väg tillsammans.


Du blir en del av ett team med djup SAP expertis där kunskapsdelning och prestigelöshet är en naturlig del av vardagen. Vi investerar i din kompetensutveckling och erbjuder hybridarbete och frihet under ansvar.


Vi lever våra kärnvärden nyfikenhet, resultat och omtanke med våra kunder och kollegor i vardagen.

**Nyfiken på oss?**


Vi är nyfikna på dig! Hör av dig så berättar vi mer om rollen, kunderna och hur det är att vara en del av Stretch.

*Hälsar teamet på Stretch Evolve i Göteborg*

title: Senior SAP Finanskonsult
description:
Finansavdelningen är hjärtat i varje bolag, men när siffrorna inte stämmer, processerna haltar och rapporteringen kräver manuella genvägar, då uppstår problemen snabbt.


Du känner igen det. Och du vet hur man får det att fungera, både i processen och i systemet.


Det är den erfarenheten vi letar efter.

**Om rollen**


Som finanskonsult hos oss jobbar du nära kunden med att få deras finansflöden att fungera i praktiken.


Du förstår hela finansflödet, från redovisning och controlling till produktkostnad, tillgångshantering och rapportering och du vet vad som behöver förändras för att systemet ska bli ett stöd, snarare än något man jobbar runt.


Du växlar mellan dialog med ekonomer och att själv vara inne i detaljerna – konfigurera, testa och säkerställa att lösningen faktiskt fungerar.


Du tar naturligt plats som rådgivare och diskussionspartner till finansavdelningen, leder fit-to-standard workshops och designar lösningar som håller på sikt, i både public och private cloud.

**Exempel på uppdrag du kan komma att jobba med**

* Delta i S/4HANA-migrationer från ECC hos multinationella bolag, ansvara för finansdesign, datakonvertering och go-live
* Agera lösningsarkitekt inom finans och designa end-to-end finansarkitektur för transformation av finansprocesser
* Arbeta i globala SAP-utrullningar med lokal anpassning till legala krav i flera länder
* Designa och implementera produktkostnadslösningar för tillverkande kunder
* Leda fit-standard workshops och bygga globala SAP-templates för finansområdet i cloud
* Stödja CFO:er och finansorganisationer som rådgivare i förändrings- och implementationsprojekt
* Arbeta med controlling, variansanalys och rapportförbättringar hos kunder i förvaltningsfas

**Vi tror att du**

* Har bred och djup erfarenhet av SAP FI/CO och finansprocesser i S/4HANA
* Är trygg i hur lösningar sätts upp i systemet, inte bara hur de borde fungera
* Har ett starkt systemintresse och gillar att få detaljerna att fungera i praktiken
* Förstår hur finans samspelar med angränsande områden som logistik, produktion och projekt
* Har erfarenhet från implementationsprojekt och vet vad som brukar bli utmanande
* Trivs i dialog med kund och kan förklara komplexa saker enkelt
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska
* Är modig, driven och prestigelös

*Meriterande*: erfarenhet av SAP Group Reporting, Treasury, PS, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Central Finance, CIM eller VIM.

**Varför Stretch Evolve?**


Hos oss försvinner du inte i mängden. Vi är ett partnerägt bolag med en platt organisation, din röst hörs och du kan påverka på riktigt: i kundprojekt, affärsutveckling och vart vi är på väg tillsammans.


Du blir en del av ett team med djup SAP-expertis där kunskapsdelning och prestigelöshet är en naturlig del av vardagen. Vi investerar i din kompetensutveckling och erbjuder hybridarbete och frihet under ansvar.


Vi lever våra kärnvärden nyfikenhet, resultat och omtanke med våra kunder och kollegor i vardagen.

**Nyfiken på oss?**


Vi är nyfikna på dig! Hör av dig så berättar vi mer om rollen, kunderna och hur det är att vara en del av Stretch.

title: Cloud Security Engineer
description:
*Nordnet redefined the financial world as the first digital bank in Europe. Now we are about to do it again and the aim is to create the next generation of bank, in the cloud. We know that this requires great people, great teams, and great technology. Do you want to join us and build the new Nordnet?*

*At Nordnet we want to democratize savings and investments by giving our customers access to the best tools and information whether you are a professional investor or a small saver. With modern technologies and speed of delivery, we are building the next generation investment platform.*

**This is part of the role**


Nordnet is a company with tech as the primary focus. In our agile and autonomous teams, you will be working with engaged colleagues that love to share their knowledge. As a cloud security engineer, you will drive automation and integrate DevSecOps into our cloud-based platform by embedding security in Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, enhancing infrastructure security and ensuring secure software development practices.

**As a Cloud Security Engineer acting autonomously and with a clear mandate, you will:**

* Develop and enforce cloud and Infrastructure As Code (IaC) security policies.
* Improve cloud security monitoring by building and managing detections.
* Collaborate with engineering teams to ensure security best practices and Secure Software Development Life Cycle SSDLC (Shift-Left).
* Facilitate threat modelling sessions, secure code review and vulnerability assessments.
* Coordinate penetration testing efforts and track remediations.
* Enable developers to ship fast by automating security
* Balance security rigor with business agility; we don't just secure, we enable.

**This is you**


To succeed in this role, we believe that you take great pride in your work, are curious and continuously want to learn and grow. Believing in collaboration and discussing ideas and concepts with your colleague, you will serve as the first point of contact for our Platform teams as a Cloud Security Subject Matter Expert to enable the delivery of great products.

**As a Cloud Security Engineer, you have:**

* Offensive security mindset ideally with former red teamer or penetration tester experience
* Knowledge of Google Cloud Platform (or equivalent experience with other Cloud providers)


	+ Google Security Command Centre (SCC)
	+ Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
	+ Identity and Access Management (IAM)
	+ Infrastructure As Code (Terraform)
	+ Github
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team
* Strong stakeholder management skills, ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
* Ability to define and implement security requirements in cloud environments
* Experience with security best practices and vulnerability management process.
* Familiarity with programming and major frameworks (Java, Spring, React)

**What we offer**

* Your expertise and contribution will be appreciated from day one
* Access to the latest AI tools with cutting edge models to support your daily work.
* Plenty of opportunities to grow as a security professional
* Competitive salary and compensation
* Opportunity to shape the architecture, influencing the security design of Europe’s next-generation digital bank from the ground up.
* Access to training opportunities such as professional certifications (e.g., GCP Security, OSCP, OSAI+, CISSP), industry conferences, and dedicated time for skills development.
* You won't just follow a checklist; you’ll have the mandate to build and own your security automation roadmap.


We offer you the opportunity to work in a Nordic environment with a strong focus on delivery, product development and technology. Our ambitions are high and you will embark on a fast and challenging journey together with a skillful team of sharp and committed colleagues. Our teams are autonomous and embrace the agile way of working.


Culture is built and cared for, each day by everyone. We’re proud of ours. Having a flat organization where anyone can talk to anyone creates a warm and friendly atmosphere worth protecting. We believe in a culture where every effort counts and where everyone is being recognized. A culture embracing our core values – passion, simplicity and transparency on all levels, no matter who you are or what you do.


We are over 900 employees located in Stockholm, Oslo, Helsinki, Copenhagen and Frankfurt.  

  

Visit us on: career.nordnetab.com/pages/engineering  

Learn more about Nordnet in our brand movie This is Nordnet, that describes our identity.  

  

**Questions & Applications**  

  

**Please note** that we will start reviewing applications after the summer holidays. To ensure a fair process and that your data is handled securely, we only accept applications through our recruitment system, applications sent via email will not be considered.  

  

If you have questions, please contact Hiring Manager Ralph Benton at ralph.benton@nordnet.se  

  

We want to inform you that Nordnet conducts mandatory credit and background checks, as well as drug testing as a part of our recruitment process.  

  

We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Event Manager
description:
**Job Title:** Event Manager


**Reports to:** Interim Head of Marketing  

**Department:** Marketing
  

**Location:** Gothenburg hybrid, Sweden  

  

**About The Job**  

We are looking for an Event Manager to lead how Avinode Group shows up at the events that matter most to our business, our members, and the wider business aviation industry.
  

This is a strategic, commercially driven role with a clear hands-on dimension. You will shape our global event program, work closely with Sales and Commercial leadership, and make sure every event we attend has a clear purpose, measurable impact, and a strong Avinode presence.
  

You will join a warm, collaborative Marketing team and work across Commercial, Product, and other key functions. The right person brings structure to complexity, builds trust quickly, and enjoys turning big plans into well-executed experiences that create real value.
  

  

**What You Will Be Doing**  

As Event Manager, you will own Avinode Group’s external event program, including industry conferences, trade shows, member events, and our own flagship gatherings. You will be responsible for the strategy, planning, execution, budget, and follow-up that make each event count. You will be the custodian of our event framework, helping us choose where to invest our time and resources. Our events should help us strengthen relationships with existing members, create and develop new leads in key markets, and stay close to industry trends, competitor activity, and customer needs. You will make sure every event we attend can be clearly connected to at least one of these goals.
  

  

You will manage our three-tier event model, deciding how we show up depending on the opportunity. Some events will call for a highly visible, sponsored presence where we make a strong brand statement and generate commercial momentum. Others will require targeted thought leadership, focused brand support, or a leaner presence designed for relationship-building and industry visibility.
  

  

A big part of this role is making sure our event program is closely connected to Sales. You will partner with Commercial leadership to decide which events we attend, define what success looks like before we commit, and make sure Sales has what they need before, during, and after each event. Your work will help turn event presence into pipeline, stronger relationships, and revenue opportunities.
  

  

You will also bring discipline to how we measure success. Together with Sales and Marketing, you will set clear KPIs, report consistently on results, and lead structured retrospectives after key events. You will capture what worked, what could be better, and what we should do differently next time, helping the program become sharper with every cycle.
  

This is a hands-on role as well as a strategic one. You will align stakeholders, manage timelines, keep planning moving, make confident budget decisions, and step in operationally when needed. You will balance large flagship investments with lean, high-return attendance and help Avinode show up in a way that feels thoughtful, professional, and commercially relevant.
  

  

**What You Will Bring Along**  

You have solid experience leading B2B events and trade shows, ideally with ownership of an event program, budget, or both. You understand that events are not only about logistics, but about relationships, positioning, pipeline, and business impact.
  

  

You are commercially minded and enjoy working closely with Sales. You know how to connect event activity to revenue goals, and you are comfortable setting KPIs, tracking outcomes, and using data to improve future plans.
  

  

You are structured, communicative, and calm in a busy environment. You can align stakeholders across functions, manage competing priorities, and keep people informed without losing sight of the bigger picture. You are also a practical problem-solver who is happy to move between strategy and execution.
  

We believe you will thrive in this role if you bring:

* 5-7+ years of experience organizing and leading B2B events, trade shows, or similar commercial event programs.
* A proven ability to work closely with Sales or Commercial teams.
* Strong stakeholder-management skills and confidence working with leaders across functions.
* Excellent project-management skills in a fast-moving environment.
* Experience setting, tracking, and reporting on event KPIs.
* Fluent English and strong communication skills.
* Comfort working independently and as part of a team.
* The ability to manage both large and tight budgets.
* Willingness to travel occasionally.
 **What Do We Offer You?**  

We are proud to offer a workplace with high engagement, where creativity, curiosity, and good ideas are always appreciated. At Avinode, your voice will be heard, and you will have room to shape how we work.

  

We offer flexible work locations and the freedom to plan your calendar, competitive salary and benefits, and opportunities to grow through training and learning. You will also be part of our Synergy events, where Noders from across the company come together to collaborate, share knowledge, and have fun, which we think matters too.
  

You will join a company with a small-company feel and a global footprint, where your work is visible and where you can make a real impact on how we connect with members, prospects, and the business aviation community.
  

  

**Want To Join Our Crew?**  

Sounds like a great fit? You can find more fun facts about our Noders, that’s what we call ourselves, and our culture on our website.
  

Please apply through the apply button with your CV and cover letter in English as soon as possible. We are not able to accept applications by email. Selections will run continuously, so please do not wait to apply.
  

  

**About Avinode Group**  

Avinode Group offers a fully connected suite of essential software that supports business aviation professionals through the full workflow of managing, sourcing, and booking aircraft. Founded in the heart of Scandinavia in 2002, we now support more than 1,800 members globally, with teams across Europe, the US, and Australia.

  

Our tools are designed to minimize stress and maximize profit for brokers and operators. The suite includes the world’s leading sourcing platform for air charter, making it easy to find aircraft, book trips, and market aircraft globally. Air charter operators and flight departments use our fully cloud-based fleet management system to make flight operations smoother, and our integrated payment solution supports a seamless process from quoting to booking and paying.
  

  

Avinode Group is an equal opportunity employer. We care about your skills, your experience, and who you are as a person.

title: AI Engineer
description:
Join Dustin and build AI systems that run real business processes and deliver measurable impact from day one. In this role, you’ll turn complex operational challenges into production-ready AI agents that automate work, improve decisions, and transform how the business operates.

**This is what makes the position as AI Engineer unique**

* Build AI agents that directly transform core business processes, delivering measurable operational impact across Dustin.
* Work hands-on with cutting-edge agentic AI technology using Python, Azure AI Foundry, and the Microsoft Agentic Framework in real production environments.
* Turn real business problems into intelligent automations by designing agent behavior, orchestration logic, and end-to-end AI-driven workflows.  

  

The Hiring Manager for the role is **Anna Zalamans Akkawi, Manager- Performance & Data Insights**. As a leader, her approach is built on trust, growth, and open dialogue, creating an environment where we collaborate closely, challenge ideas, and support each other to succeed as a team.

**Your role & influence as AI Engineer**

*Responsibility*: You will be given responsibility for designing and building agentic AI solutions that automate and optimize core business processes by defining agent behavior, orchestration logic, and end-to-end workflow execution.

*Collaboration:* Together with your team, you take care of delivering AI-driven automation that solves real operational challenges and creates measurable business value. You'll work closely not only with your immediate team members but also with the business stakeholders to ensure solutions are relevant, effective, and impactful*.*

*Core activities:*

* Design and develop agentic AI solutions using Python and the Microsoft Agentic Framework to solve real business problems.
* Translate business processes and pain points into functional AI agents, workflows, and automation logic.
* Collaborate with stakeholders to validate solutions, refine requirements, and ensure delivered AI systems create measurable impact.
* Ensure all solutions adhere to **AI governance, GDPR, and EU AI Act requirements**, aligned with AI Central of Excellence standards

**Benefits of joining Dustin**


We believe there's more to Dustin than just the benefits listed here. You'll fully understand this once you're with us. Curious? Our Life at Dustin page has you covered.

**You can find more of our Swedish benefits below in the vacancy.**

**This is what we imagine you bring**


We envision you as a curious and driven individual who enjoys solving real business problems by turning them into practical, production-ready AI solutions that create measurable impact.


Additionally, it's important that:

* You have a bachelor's degree level of working and thinking
* You have at least 3+ years or equivalent experience in software engineering, ideally in environments involving cloud-based or AI-driven solutions
* Experience with software engineering in production environments, preferably involving cloud platforms (Azure is a plus) and close collaboration with business stakeholders
* Technical skills in Python, cloud platforms (preferably Azure), and building AI or agent-based systems, with the ability to design workflows, automation logic, and production-ready applications
* You have a good understanding of CI/CD principles, Git-based workflows, and production deployment practices, with experience working in environments where these are managed by dedicated platform or infrastructure teams.

**What can you expect next?**


To ensure a fair recruitment process free from unconscious bias, we handle your application anonymously and do not accept cover letters. Please refrain from sending one, so that the selection is based solely on professional competencies. We expect the following steps in the recruitment process:

* Telephone screening with TA Partner , Karishma
* First Interview with the Hiring Manager, Anna and AI & Innovation Lead, Marcel
* Second/ Technical Interview with other set of colleagues
* Professional References and Background check & verification.

***Please note that for this specific job, we don´t provide any relocation or work permit sponsorship support.***

***We encourage you to apply and express your interest. However, due to the summer holiday period, there will be a delay in our response.***  

  

Read more about how to become a Dustineer
company_description: We are a leading online based IT partner in the Nordics and the Benelux. We are in constant movement and with that comes many opportunities for us to develop and grow together. Are you ready to join?

title: Junior Platform Engineer
description:
Build something monumental for Healthcare!
   

  

 At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.
   

  

 We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You enjoy making systems work, not just writing code, but understanding how everything fits together underneath. You’ve probably spent time tinkering on your own, setting up servers, hosting things yourself, or trying to understand how infrastructure actually behaves in the real world.
   

  

 At Tandem, you’ll join a small, highly skilled platform team and help build the infrastructure that powers the future of European healthcare. You don’t need to know everything from day one - what matters most is your drive to learn, your curiosity, and your willingness to get your hands dirty.
   

  

 This role is focused on infrastructure first. You’ll work closely with experienced engineers, gradually taking ownership of parts of our platform and helping the team move faster by offloading and improving core infrastructure work. Along the way, you’ll learn how to build systems that are reliable, secure, and scalable in a highly regulated environment.
   

  

 If you’re someone who enjoys figuring things out, breaking and fixing systems, and continuously improving how things run - this is a great place to grow.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Support the team in building and maintaining cloud infrastructure that powers Tandem’s products.
* Work with Kubernetes-based systems and help improve how services are deployed and operated.
* Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, making deployments smoother and more reliable.
* Assist in implementing infrastructure-as-code using tools like Terraform and Helm.
* Help improve observability, monitoring, and alerting across our systems.
* Take ownership of smaller infrastructure components over time and help scale them.
* Work closely with senior engineers to learn best practices around reliability, security, and performance.


**What You Bring**
* Some experience working with cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, or GCP).
* Familiarity with Kubernetes, CI/CD, or infrastructure tooling - even if not at an expert level.
* Exposure to infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform or Helm.
* Basic programming skills - enough to script, automate, and understand systems.
* A strong curiosity for how systems work under the hood.
* A mindset of learning and improvement - you’re eager to develop your skills quickly.
* Interest in building reliable systems that help others move faster.


**Bonus Points**
* You’ve built or run your own infrastructure projects (e.g. self-hosting, home labs, media servers, etc.)
* Experience working with slightly larger systems or production environments
* Familiarity with networking, security, or observability concepts
* Experience with Kubernetes packaging (e.g. Helm charts)
* Background in software engineering or scripting-heavy roles


 Location
   

  

 We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.
   

  

**Our Interview Process Consists Of 4 Stages**
* Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
* Technical interviews with some of our Engineers
* Interview with our CTO & Co-Founder
* Home case and presentation at our office - Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch, a presentation to experience our our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.


**Benefits**
* Competitive salary & company stock options
* 25 days/year of paid vacation
* Pension
* 5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag)
* Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
* An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
* Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


 Culture at Tandem
   

  

 At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.
   

  

 Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.
   

  

 We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.
   

  

*Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.*

title: Solutions Engineer
description:
**Business Area:**
 Sales Engineering
   

  

**Seniority Level:**
 Mid-Senior level
   

  

**Job Description:**
 At Cloudera, we empower people to transform complex data into clear and actionable insights. With as much data under management as the hyperscalers, we're the preferred data partner for the top companies in almost every industry. Powered by the relentless innovation of the open source community, Cloudera advances digital transformation for the world’s largest enterprises.
   

  

 Cloudera Solutions Engineers are the technical leaders in the sales account team. We’re a close-knit community and truly enthusiastic about our work, which gives us the opportunity to grow our personal reputation both internally and externally in the data community. We are responsible for securing the technical win in each sales opportunity and for building an ongoing relationship with our customers to ensure their business success with Cloudera technologies.
   

  

****As a Solutions Engineer you will:****
* Own the technical sales process from introductory meetings (net new sales) through post-sales (customer satisfaction, upselling, and subscription renewals)
* Supporting the technical needs of customers including discovering new use cases for Cloudera’s technology
* Designing solutions for your customers’ needs using Cloudera technologies, based on reference architectures and common patterns
* Demonstrating Cloudera products in a way that inspires your customers to say “yes” to Cloudera
* Partnering with your Account Managers to show your customers the true value of a Cloudera solution and drive successful sales
* Presenting product roadmap and vision to inspire your customers to think bigger
* Interfacing with other Cloudera teams to ensure your customers hear one Cloudera voice
* Advocating for your customers’ needs to Product Management and Engineering
* Participating in external publicity and evangelism (conferences, meetups, webinars, blogs)


 Achievements are aligned to a team goal with annual sales targets, as well as to your participation in knowledge exchange with peers and the wider community.
   

  

****We're excited about you if you have:****
* A passion for using technology to make a difference to people and organisations. We'll supply some amazing tools to help you with this and you'll provide the connection to use cases, which make it real for your customers
* A minimum of 3 years experience in a customer-facing role, ideally in a pre-sales context
* Bachelor's degree in related field of study or equivalent work experience
* Valid Security Clearance (Level 2 at the minimum)
* The ability to create and give technical presentations and demonstrations
* Experience with some of the following technologies:
+ Big Data, Data Warehousing or Relational Databases
+ One or more of the three major cloud service providers (AWS, Azure or GCP). Formal certification is not required, but would be a great thing to have
+ Data Engineering, Machine Learning and/or AI
+ Operations, Security and Data Governance within the enterprise
+ Unix or Linux

* Experience gathering and understanding customer business requirements
* Excellent written and verbal communication
* Problem solving skills
* A willingness to learn, a curiosity to discover and a drive to make your customers successful


**What you can expect from us:**
* Generous PTO Policy
* Support work life balance with Unplugged Days
* Flexible WFH Policy
* Mental & Physical Wellness programs
* Phone and Internet Reimbursement program
* Access to Continued Career Development
* Comprehensive Benefits and Competitive Packages
* Paid Volunteer Time
* Employee Resource Groups


 EEO/VEVRAA

title: Integrations- och Lösningsarkitekt –
description:
**Startdatum:** 
 September
   

  

**Slutdatum:** 
 Option på förlängning)
   

  

**Omfattning:** 
 100%
   

  

**Plats:** 
 Stockholm / Hybrid
   

  

**Uppdragsbeskrivning**
 Vi söker en senior integrations- och lösningsarkitekt till ett långsiktigt uppdrag hos en större offentlig verksamhet. Rollen innebär att arbeta nära verksamhet och utvecklingsteam med vidareutveckling av ett affärskritiskt systemlandskap där integrationer utgör en central del av den tekniska lösningen.
   

  

 Du blir en viktig del av ett tvärfunktionellt agilt team och ansvarar för att utforma hållbara integrationslösningar, säkerställa teknisk arkitektur samt bidra till modernisering av en etablerad plattform inför framtida tekniska förändringar.
   

  

 Uppdraget omfattar både strategiskt arkitekturarbete och operativt stöd inom integration, infrastruktur och teknisk design.
   

  

**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter**
* Ta fram och vidareutveckla lösningsarkitektur för komplexa integrationslösningar.
* Designa och dokumentera integrationer mellan verksamhetskritiska system.
* Säkerställa att lösningar följer målarkitektur och tekniska riktlinjer.
* Arbeta nära utvecklingsteam, arkitekter och verksamhet i ett agilt arbetssätt.
* Hantera tekniska plattformar, certifikat, nätverks- och infrastrukturrelaterade frågor.
* Bidra till modernisering och framtida utveckling av systemlandskapet.


**Skall-krav**
* Minst 10 års erfarenhet som lösningsarkitekt, varav minst 8 år inom integrationsarkitektur.
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet av integrationslösningar baserade på MuleSoft Anypoint Platform.
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet av integrationsarbete kopplat till Microsoft Dynamics CRM.
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet av integrationstestning.
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet av utveckling enligt agila arbetssätt.
* Flytande i svenska tal och skrift


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet från verksamheter inom persontransporter, mobilitet eller liknande samhällskritiska tjänster.
* Erfarenhet av Microsoft Dynamics CRM On-Premise.
* Tidigare uppdrag inom offentlig sektor.

title: Pre-Sales Architect - Sweden
description:
**OUR STORY:**
 🇪🇺 Join Scaleway and shape the sovereign cloud of tomorrow !
   

  

 Since 1999, we have been designing secure, sustainable infrastructures aimed at supporting the most ambitious companies.
   

  

 Historically known for our dedicated servers (Dedibox), we made a strategic shift to cloud computing in 2015. Staying true to our principles of simplicity, flexibility, and technical excellence, we have become one of the leading players in Europe in the sector.
   

  

 With the rise of artificial intelligence, we have strengthened our commitment, supported by the Iliad Group, which is investing €3 billion to develop a serious, sovereign AI alternative to American and Asian giants.
   

  

 Every day, thanks to our fast-growing portfolio of cloud and AI products (bare metal, containerization, serverless, AI, etc.), Scaleway proudly serves thousands of customer across the private and public sector, from corporations like France Télévisions or Hachette Livre, to fast-growing startups like Photoroom and Biolevate, to institutions like the City of Copenhagen.
   

  

 📍 Our offices are located in
 **Paris, Lille, Toulouse, Rennes, Rouen, Bordeaux and Lyon.**
**WHY WE NEED YOU?**
 Our growth is driving us to strengthen our Solutions Architect team to
 **support our commercial expansion** 
 across international markets.
   

  

 Your mission will be to
 **guide clients** 
 through their cloud journey with Scaleway, from discovery to migration, in order to
 **accelerate customer acquisition** 
 and deliver
 **tailor-made technical solutions** 
 .
   

  

**YOUR FUTURE TEAM**
 We work in a collaborative and international environment where the diversity of Scalers, combined with a spirit of sharing, helps bring new projects to life every day, advancing our ambitions together.
   

  

 You will be part of a team of experienced Pre-Sales Architects, paired with Account Executives, Inside Sales Representatives, Customer Success Managers, and Business Developers, all working together to serve strategic markets.
   

  

 You’ll operate closely with the Sweden-based sales team while remaining integrated within the global Solutions Architecture department.
   

  

**YOUR DAILY ROUTINE**
* Act as the primary technical contact during the pre-sales phase for enterprise prospects and clients
* Understand customer business needs and technical constraints to design Scaleway-based architectures
* Support clients throughout the discovery and migration journey until the project transitions to support
* Conduct in-depth technical discovery and propose custom solutions leveraging Scaleway products
* Collaborate with Sales, Product, and Engineering teams to address customer challenges
* Drive technical workshops and proof-of-concept sessions with clients
* Maintain and share knowledge on Scaleway’s evolving cloud offering
* Participate in improving internal pre-sales tools and processes
* Represent Scaleway at industry events and webinars when needed
* Contribute to the continuous improvement of the Pre-Sales practice and documentation


**Hardskills**
**ABOUT YOU**
* 3 to 10 years of experience in technical pre-sales or solution architecture roles
* Experience working with public cloud solutions and migrations
* Familiarity with container orchestration, Infrastructure as Code, or Linux systems
* Solid understanding of application/server architectures and datacenter environments
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience
* Strong communication skills in both Swedish and English


**Softskills**
* Customer-oriented with a consultative mindset
* Ability to understand and translate complex technical requirements into scalable solutions
* Excellent collaboration and stakeholder management abilities
* Comfortable presenting to both technical and C-level audiences
* Proactive, adaptable, and eager to drive innovation


**WHAT YOU WILL FIND AT SCALEWAY ++++**
**Hybrid work:** 
 We offer up to 3 days of remote work per week.
   

  

**Well-being commitments:** 
 Whether it’s access to a gym, daycare places, or discounted services for caring services, Scaleway is committed to supporting Scalers in maintaining a balanced life.
   

  

**International environment:** 
 With dozens of nationalities, Scaleway offers a stimulating environment where English is as widely spoken as French.
   

  

**Career & Mobility:** 
 Our managers value internal mobility, and opportunities to transition to other entities within the Iliad Group are accessible to all Scalers.
   

  

**🚀 Why join the Scaleway adventure?**
 ✔ A rich and diverse product offering: Scaleway offers over 100 public cloud products in IaaS, PaaS, and AI.
   

  

 ✔ A cutting-edge technical environment: Scaleway provides modern infrastructures, including high-performance bare metal servers, to tackle exciting technical challenges.
   

  

 ✔ Commitment to responsible cloud: Scaleway is dedicated to a more responsible cloud, with data centers powered solely by renewable energy since 2017, minimizing our ecological footprint and holding top-level certification.
   

  

**🔜 THE NEXT STEPS …**
 1 - Talent acquisition interview (30 min)
   

  

 2 - Head of Sales Architecture / International PSA (30 min)
   

  

 3 - Technical Interview to understand your technical skills and approach to the role (45 min)
   

  

 4 - CSO Interview
   

  

 Version française disponible ici
   

  

*At Scaleway, we are committed to building an inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone has a fair opportunity to thrive.*
*All applications are considered with care, regardless of age, gender, sexual orientation, ethnic or social background, religion, disability, or any other characteristic.*
*We believe great ideas come from everywhere, and everyone which is why you should definitely apply.*

title: Partner Solution Engineer
description:
At Snowflake
   

  

 We are powering the era of the agentic enterprise. To usher in this new era, we seek AI-native thinkers across every function who are energized by the opportunity to reinvent how they work. You don’t just use tools; you possess an innate curiosity, treating AI as a high-trust collaborator that is core to how you solve problems and accelerate your impact. We look for low-ego individuals who thrive in dynamic and fast-moving environments and move with an experimental mindset — who rapidly test emerging capabilities to discover simpler, more powerful ways to deliver results. At Snowflake, your role isn't just to execute a function, but to help redefine the future of how work gets done.
   

  

 We are looking for a Partner Solution Engineer
   

  

 Who is accustomed to working with Partners and Systems Integrators. In this role, you will work directly with our consulting & SI partners, enabling them on key platform features and their business benefits and helping them to grow their Snowflake services business and shape their go-to-market.
   

  

 As a Snowflake Partner Solution Engineer, you must share our passion for innovating through the AI Data Cloud, thrive in a dynamic environment, and have the flexibility and willingness to jump in and get things done. You are equally comfortable in both a business and technical context, interacting with executives and inspiring technical audiences.
   

  

**In This Role You Will Get To**
* Present Snowflake technology and vision to decision makers and technical contributors at partners, prospects and customers
* Work hands-on with partners and customers to demonstrate and communicate the value of Snowflake technology throughout the sales cycle
* Build technical champions within our partners and grow the local Snowflake technical community
* Immerse yourself in the ever-evolving industry, maintaining a deep understanding of competitive and complementary technologies and vendors and how to position Snowflake in relation to them.
* Collaborate with Product Management, Engineering, and Marketing to continuously improve Snowflake’s products and marketing


**On Day One We Will Expect You To Have**
* 4 years of industry experience with a minimum of 2 within a pre-sales environment working directly with consulting & System Integration (GSI/RSI) partners
* Outstanding presenting skills to both technical and executive audiences, whether impromptu on a whiteboard or using presentations and demos
* Broad range of experience within large-scale Database and/or Data Warehouse technology, ETL, analytics and cloud technologies
* Hands-on expertise with SQL
* Experience in data science is highly desirable as is hands-on Python experience
* Ability to connect specific business problems and Snowflake’s solutions
* University degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics or related fields, or equivalent experience preferred
* Fluency in English required


 Snowflake is growing fast
   

  

 And we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.
   

  

 How do you want to make your impact?

title: Bioinformatics Engineer
description:
**BIOINFORMATICS ENGINEER** 




 Metagenomics & Computational Infrastructure
 



  





**Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden
 



**Employment:** 
 Full-time position
 



**Start date:** 
 Flexible, preferably in Autumn 2026.
 



  





 GenomeVault is a NatureTech company building the next generation of biodiversity intelligence through genome-wide environmental DNA (eDNA) analysis. Our work combines molecular biology, high-throughput next-generation sequencing, metagenomics, and modern computational infrastructure to help organizations measure, monitor, and manage biodiversity.
 



 We are now looking for a full-time
 **Bioinformatics Engineer** 
 to join our growing team. This hybrid role combines software and infrastructure engineering with applied bioinformatics. Approximately 60% of the role will focus on designing, building, and maintaining GenomeVault's scientific computing infrastructure, while 40% will involve bioinformatics analysis, pipeline, and method development.
 



  





**What you’ll do** 




 You will play a central role in connecting laboratory operations with high-performance computing and bioinformatics workflows. You will help establish robust, scalable infrastructure while also contributing to the analysis of complex metagenomic datasets.
 



  





**Key responsibilities** 




***Computational infrastructure (60%)***



* Design, implement, and maintain scientific computing infrastructure.
* Integrate our Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) with automated analysis workflows.
* Develop and maintain scalable Nextflow pipelines running on HPC environments.
* Build tools and databases for tracking samples, metadata, and analysis outputs.
* Improve reproducibility, automation, and data management across the organization.
* Collaborate closely with laboratory scientists to streamline the transition from sample to result.



***Bioinformatics and data analysis (40%)***



* Analyze metagenomic sequencing data from diverse environmental samples.
* Develop and optimize workflows for assembly, taxonomic profiling, genome reconstruction, and functional annotation.
* Work with microbial and eukaryotic genomes recovered from metagenomic data.
* Contribute to method development and support research projects across the company.
* Help translate biological questions into computational solutions.



  





**Who you are** 



* Passion for biodiversity and a desire to apply science to real-world environmental challenges.
* PhD or MSc in Computer Science, Bioinformatics or a related field.
* Experience designing or integrating data systems, databases, APIs, or scientific software platforms.
* Experience developing, deploying, and maintaining scalable bioinformatics pipelines for metagenomic data analysis in research or production environments.
* Strong programming skills
* Experience with workflow management systems, particularly
 **Nextflow** 
 .
* Experience working with Linux environments and high-performance computing (HPC) systems.
* Familiarity with version control and reproducible computational practices.
* A proactive mindset toward adopting and integrating AI technologies into scientific and operational workflows, and an interest in exploring how emerging AI tools can enhance efficiency, automation, and data analysis throughout the organization.
* Ability to work independently and communicate effectively in a multidisciplinary team.



**Preferred** 



* Experience recovering and analyzing
 **eukaryotic genomes from metagenomic datasets** 
 .
* Knowledge of cloud computing and infrastructure management.
* Familiarity with container technologies such as Docker and Singularity/Apptainer.
* Experience with large-scale genomics projects and data management.



  





**What we offer** 



* Join a young, mission-driven team of scientists, engineers, and entrepreneurs working to turn cutting-edge genomics into real-world biodiversity intelligence.
* The opportunity to help build the computational foundations of a rapidly growing biotechnology company.
* A highly interdisciplinary environment combining genomics, biodiversity, and software engineering.
* Significant freedom and responsibility, with the ability to shape infrastructure and analytical strategies from the ground up.
* Collaboration with scientists and engineers working at the forefront of biodiversity and genome discovery.
* Flexible and hybrid working arrangements.
* Competitive salary, pension contributions, and the possibility to participate in the company's employee stock option program.



  





**Join us** 




 If you are excited about building the infrastructure that powers next-generation metagenomics and want to work at the interface of biology, software engineering, and biodiversity science, we would love to hear from you.
 



  





 Please send your CV and a short cover letter describing your background and interest in the position to
 **contact@genomevault.net** 
 . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

title: Staff / Principal Software Engineer, Product
description:
**TL;DR:** 
 We're seeking exceptional senior engineers and technical leaders for our Product team to help build the future of AI software creation. You will work across our entire stack to build features that will empower the next generation of creators and builders.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Lovable-built applications and websites are visited hundreds of millions of times a month, and our enterprise footprint is compounding fast. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
* 10+ years of experience designing, building, and shipping production products, with a track record operating at senior engineering levels.
* Deep experience across the full stack, or strong backend depth - fluent in modern frontend (React/TypeScript) and/or backend (Golang, Rust) development.
* Solid understanding of systems design, performance tradeoffs, and scalable architecture for products serving millions of users.
* Strong product sense and the ability to make well-reasoned technical decisions - knowing when to prioritise speed and when to invest in foundational work.
* You own systems end-to-end: architecture, implementation, deployment, monitoring, and iteration.
* You're comfortable navigating ambiguity and driving clarity at an organisational level, not just technically.


**What You’ll Do**
**In one sentence:** 
 Do all it takes to build a generational product - scaling the core Lovable experience for millions of users worldwide.
   

  

* Build and scale the systems that power Lovable products, ensuring they're fast, reliable, and ready to support millions of users.
* Implement and maintain features across the stack - frontend, backend, and infrastructure - owning them from architecture through to production.
* Design, implement, and maintain backend services, APIs, and infrastructure components built for scale.
* Collaborate closely with ML, design, and product teams to translate capabilities into intuitive, mind-blowing user experiences.
* Drive the full development lifecycle, from technical planning to production launch and post-release iteration.
* Contribute to shaping our technical direction, engineering culture, and product strategy.


**Our tech stack**
 We're building with tools that both humans and AI love:
   

  

* Frontend: React
* Backend: Golang and Rust
* Cloud: Cloudflare, GCP, AWS, Many LLM providers

 And always on the lookout for what's next!
   

  

**About Your Application**

 We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested, please apply through our careers portal.

title: Channel Sales Manager - Northern Europe (Civil Construction)
description:
**Your Title:** 
 Channel Sales Manager - Northern Europe
   

  

**Job Location:** 
 Sweden - Danderyd or Remote in Sweden, Norway, Finland
   

  

**Division:** 
 Civil Construction Field Solutions CCFS
   

  

*Are you looking to be a part of an award-winning organization and one of the fastest growing technology companies that is transforming the construction landscape with innovative technology solutions?*
*Join our team! We will offer your versatile tasks in a diverse, inspiring and dynamic working environment with great colleagues. Apply as soon as possible! Please, submit your CV/application only in English.*
 In this position you will have a very specific focus on CEC Machine control solutions and Connected Site promotion with a primary focus on Northern Europe. You will be deeply involved in developing solid business relationships with Equipment Dealers.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 You will provide sales and application support for distributors and potentially end users in the CEC product portfolio You will:
   

  

* Increase market share through the development of Connected Site solutions to existing accounts and new customers. You will also be able to develop customer interest on Trimble Continuum solutions to develop sales on the entire CEC solutions
* Sell through select and perhaps multiple channels of distribution and have large project involvement, and will develop strategic key end-user relationships to ensure the successful sale of all Trimble products and services. You will have a strong field presence across the Nordic region
* Be responsible for ongoing dealer development through the introduction, follow-up and ongoing involvement in Trimble’s channel development programs/workshops.


**Primary Responsibilities**
 You will report to the Nordic Regional Sales Manager. Together with the Regional Sales Manager, you will participate in the development and implementation of regionally assigned sales plans. You will be in interface with distributor management, operations, sales, service and support staff.
   

  

**You Will**
* Develop strong long term business development with regional equipment dealers and implementation of Trimble Technology Outlets
* Conduct on site distributor training and job site demonstrations together with the application engineers
* Be in interface with major regional accounts
* Conduct joint sales presentations with focus on Educational Presentations
* Drive the Annual Review Meetings with key accounts in each dealer territory
* Assist in the identification, procurement and subsequent training of distribution sales and application personnel
* Evaluate marketplace potential for all served markets and customer segments using Data Analytics services as available
* Assist in the development of and participation in, specific technical communications and training requirements such as Trimble Dimensions, Boot Camps, Construction News, and others
* Ensure new product release involvement by all dealers including local strategy, training and proper inventory
* Provide input on competitive products and activities primarily to the CCFS Business Area
* Assist in the identification and development of new distribution.


**What Skills, Experience And Knowledge You Should Bring**
* Proven experience of successful consultative sales
* Experience in Channel development and management
* Business, sales, market and domain knowledge in survey, civil engineering and / or construction
* Ideally experienced in Technology solutions for Construction
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Ability and willingness to travel up to+/- 50%
* Proficient knowledge of civil construction applications
* Product knowledge is a bonus
* Strong collaboration and relationship building skills
* Ability to prioritize, organize and manage multiple projects at one time
* Fluency in English and one Scandinavian language. Additional Nordic language skills are a bonus.


**About Our CCFS Division**
 Trimble's Civil Construction Field Solutions division focuses on providing advanced technology solutions to enhance productivity, accuracy, and efficiency in civil construction projects. This division integrates hardware and software tools to support the entire construction lifecycle, from initial site assessment and planning through to design, construction, and ongoing maintenance.
   

  

 Key offerings include machine control systems, site positioning systems, and software for project management and collaboration. These tools enable precise grading, excavation, paving, and site management, which help contractors and engineers streamline workflows, reduce rework, and improve overall project outcomes. For more information on currently developed products, see https://heavyindustry.trimble.com
   

  

**About Trimble**
 Dedicated to the world’s tomorrow, Trimble is a technology company delivering solutions that enable our customers to work in new ways to measure, build, grow and move goods for a better quality of life. Core technologies in positioning, modeling, connectivity and data analytics connect the digital and physical worlds to improve productivity, quality, safety, transparency and sustainability. From purpose-built products and enterprise lifecycle solutions to industry cloud services, Trimble is transforming critical industries such as construction, geospatial, agriculture and transportation to power an interconnected world of work. For more information about Trimble (NASDAQ: TRMB), visit: https://www.trimble.com/en
   

  

**Trimble’s Inclusiveness Commitment**
 We believe in celebrating our differences. That is why our diversity is our strength. To us, that means actively participating in opportunities to be inclusive. Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion have guided our current success while also moving our desire to improve. We actively seek to add members to our community who represent our customers and the places we live and work.
   

  

 We have programs in place to make sure our people are seen, heard, and welcomed and most importantly that they know they belong, no matter who they are or where they are coming from.
   

  

**Trimble’s Privacy Policy**
****How to Apply:****
 Please submit an online application for this position by clicking on the ‘Apply Now’ button located in this posting.
   

  

****Join a Values-Driven Team: Belong, Grow, Innovate.****
 At Trimble, our core values of Belong, Grow, and Innovate aren't just words—they're the foundation of our culture. We foster an environment where you are seen, heard, and valued (Belong); where you have an opportunity to build a career and drive our collective growth (Grow); and where your innovative ideas shape the future (Innovate). We believe in empowering local teams to create impactful strategies, ensuring our global vision resonates with every individual. Become part of a team where your contributions truly matter.
   

  

 Trimble’s Privacy Policy
   

  

 If you need assistance or would like to request an accommodation in connection with the application process, please contact AskPX@px.trimble.com.

title: Senior ASIC/FPGA Engineer
description:
Help build the hardware core of a technology that makes computing faster, leaner, and more energy-efficient - at scale.
  
The role
  
You'll own complex RTL development end-to-end, from architectural trade-offs and early exploration through synthesis, timing closure, and FPGA-based validation. Working closely with software teams, you'll adapt and harden ASIC-targeted designs for FPGA implementation, analyze implementation results, and drive measurable improvements in performance, power, and area. This is a high-ownership position with real impact on the efficiency of computing hardware.
  
  

What you bring
  
Data compression in hardware — hands-on depth with compression algorithms applied in an RTL or hardware-adjacent context, not just theoretical familiarity.
  
Production-grade SystemVerilog — you write and maintain synthesizable RTL at a level that goes directly into real designs, not just simulations.
  
Synthesis & timing closure — solid, practical experience with RTL synthesis, low-power design techniques, and meeting PPA targets across technology nodes.
  
On-chip protocols — practical working knowledge of CHI, AXI, or comparable memory and interconnect interface standards.
  
Scripting & automation — Tcl and Python used in real design, verification, or automation workflows.
  
C for hardware modeling — C used for validation, behavioral modeling, or internal tooling alongside HDL work.
  
  

How we work
  
Close collaboration between hardware and software — you're the technical bridge, not a handoff point.
  
**Research-driven environment:** ambiguity is expected, and so is the judgment to navigate it toward robust outcomes.
  
Knowledge-sharing is part of the job — both contributing to and building up team capability over time.
  
**Core values:** creativity, innovation, and teamwork.

title: Senior Software Integration Engineer – AUTOSAR [F/M/X]
description:
Job description
  
  

We are looking for a Senior Software Integration Engineer to join an exciting automotive software development project focused on next-generation electric vehicle platforms.
  
  

You will play a key role in developing and integrating embedded software for advanced electronic control units (ECUs), supporting the full development lifecycle from concept and software integration to production and field support.
  
  

**Location:** Gothenburg, Sweden
  
**Experience:** 7–10 years
  
**️ Required Skills:** AUTOSAR | Python | CI/CD | Fluent English
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

Configure and integrate AUTOSAR Basic Software (BSW).
  
Plan and execute software integration, testing, and verification activities.
  
Define test strategies and ensure high software quality.
  
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for automated software integration and testing.
  
Support software architecture and integration activities.
  
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, suppliers, and technical stakeholders.
  
Contribute to software releases and configuration management.
  
Drive continuous improvement in software quality and development processes.
  
  

Requirements
  
  

7–10 years of experience in embedded software integration, testing, or verification.
  
Strong expertise in AUTOSAR and BSW configuration.
  
Experience with Python scripting and automation.
  
Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines.
  
Knowledge of embedded software development and software release processes.
  
Experience with configuration management tools.
  
Strong communication skills and fluent English.
  
  

Nice to Have
  
  

CAN and Automotive Ethernet
  
CANoe
  
Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testing
  
Real-time simulation
  
Embedded platform development
  
Continuous Integration and Continuous Testing
  
System verification and validation
  
Experience defining technical roadmaps and software evolution plans
  
  

What we offer
  
  

An international community bringing together 110+ different nationalities
  
**An environment where trust has a central place:** 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities
  
A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules
  
A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
  
At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support.
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
  
  

Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!

title: Data Platform Engineer
description:
**TL;DR** 
 - We are looking for data engineers to design, build, and maintain the end-to-end declarative data platform that powers ingestion, transformation, and consumption across Lovable. You are obsessed about empowering teams to ensure data systems are scalable, reliable, safe and easy to use.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Lovable-built applications and websites are visited hundreds of millions of times a month, and our enterprise footprint is compounding fast. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What we are looking for:**

* Frontend: React
* Backend: Golang and Rust

 And always on the lookout for what's next!
